title: Measure and meet the timing budget
description: Establish worst-case execution and latency by measurement, not estimate, and prove every deadline holds under stress.
body: Steps: 1. List each real-time path and its deadline (ISR latency, task period, end-to-end response). 2. Instrument with GPIO toggles or cycle counters and capture on a logic analyzer. 3. Drive worst-case load — maximum interrupt rate, all tasks active — and record max, not average, timing. 4. Compare measured worst case against each budget with margin. 5. If a deadline is missed, shorten ISRs, re-prioritize, or move work, then re-measure. Rollback point: before changing task priorities. Validation: every path's measured worst case fits its budget under stress; an average-case measurement or a single quiet-bench run does not prove a hard deadline.
